scores.materiality_reasoning: Cavalry eligibility implies access to specific social roles and military equipment.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to military organization and class-based service.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Service eligibility lasts as long as one's age and wealth status permit.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural birth-status in the Athenian context.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian (2nd c. CE) writes a dialogue set in the time of Solon (6th c. BCE).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a factual description of the state's military composition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Cavalry service is a specific civic role, not the definition of citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to bear arms affects physical capabilities and carries legal penalties.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad legal prohibition affecting all citizens in public spaces.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The prohibition is a permanent feature of peacetime civil law.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to archaic/classical Athenian laws from a later Roman era perspective.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicitly cites a prescribed penalty in the law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently mean one cannot carry a weapon.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to training facilities is a social practice with physical outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the institution of the gymnasium.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Gender-based exclusion from public life was lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is a biologically fixed/immutable category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Characters in the dialogue discuss classical Athenian customs.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Inferred from a factual contrast between male and female physical states.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lack of gymnasium access is not the definition of being a woman.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Athletic honors include prizes and tangible social standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scoped specifically to the context of athletic competitions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tied to the specific life stage of youth/military age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age (at a specific moment) is a biologically fixed fact.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical fiction set in the distant past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes an institutionalized system of public prizes.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Minors are not defined by their participation in games.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to personal safety and preservation of life is a tangible bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Addresses a broad protection from lethal violence in training.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource is attached to the phase of military service/training.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Soldiers are people in a specific role/active duty stage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Depicts Solon's era laws from a Roman period perspective.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Solon reports this as a rationale within the legal framework.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Soldiers are not defined by protection from lethal training.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical flogging and death are extreme bodily material harms.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific ritual (Diamastigosis) at one altar.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Limited to the transition from youth to manhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age and heritage in the Spartan context are immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The Spartan ritual was actively practiced and known in Lucian's time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a notorious and observable Spartan institution.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a minor does not inherently mean being flogged.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a pedagogical spectacle intended to alter character.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific to watching cock-fights as training.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Requirement lasts for the duration of military age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Soldiers on active duty / military age is role-bound.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Set in archaic Athens, long before Lucian.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the law 'requires' them to present themselves.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Soldiering is not defined by bird-watching.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A stipend of 10,000 drachmas is direct wealth/income.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to the specific professional chair of a philosophical sect.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Typically held for life or the duration of a career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Philosopher is a durable identity acquired through training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes a contemporary Roman imperial endowment.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Relates the clear factual details of an imperial allowance.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a philosopher does not automatically mean receiving a stipend.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Archonship chairs provide direct material and political status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Relates to eligibility for public office and assembly access.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eunuch status is a lifelong physical condition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Eunuch status was a permanent, non-reversible physical state.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects a legal dispute current to the author's world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a speech by an advocate in a judicial dispute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Eunuchs are not defined by exclusion from philosophy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The governance of boys is a social role involving authority and career.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scoped to the specific profession of teaching boys.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility lasts throughout a male adult's professional life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sexual/physical gender is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary requirements for philosophical chairs.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the arguments used by judges in a trial.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Instruction of boys is not part of the definition of 'man'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Immunity from criminal prosecution for adultery.
scores.generality_reasoning: Relates to a specific but common legal offense.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eunuchism is a permanent physical status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Eunuchism is an immutable physical condition.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a recent/relevant court case.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a factual legal strategy and outcome from a trial.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Adultery usually requires male capacity; exclusion logically follows the definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Slavery is the total loss of material and bodily freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bondage is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Enslavement for debt was typically permanent without manumission.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Economic status (poverty) is changeable but often durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes a pre-Solonian law from the distant past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports historical legal customs as plain facts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Poverty does not inherently define one as a slave.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to hold high office involves significant political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Office-holding is a broad category of social status.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Status allows entry into the lifelong Areopagus council.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Birth (nobility) and wealth class were structural and hereditary.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle describes the early Athenian constitution.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of constitutional qualifications.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Nobility does not mean 'office holder' by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The franchise (vote) has direct political and material impact.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to vote is a foundational societal right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Depends on maintaining military equipment status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ability to furnish armor is based on changeable wealth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes Draco's constitution from the 7th c. BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Relates the specific terms of the franchise factualy.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Weapon ownership does not define the act of voting.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for the archonship is a major political right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Office-holding is a broad class of outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility holds as long as the wealth threshold is met.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth ownership (10 minas) is a durable economic status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes historical Draconian ordinances.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific financial requirements for office as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Owning 10 minas does not mean 'archon' by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a social role (magistrate) through the lottery.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to many offices/magistracies.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The eligibility holds for the remainder of adult life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being 'over thirty' is a birth-fixed temporal fact.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports historical archaic/classical regulations.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the legal age-limit as a plain fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being 30 is not the definition of a magistrate.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Financial penalties (fines) are direct loss of wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scoped to the specific fine for council absence.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to a single procedure/absence.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Property classes were durable but potentially changeable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports historical Solonian/post-Solonian laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Lists specific fine amounts for each class as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealthy status does not define the payment of fines.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from enslavement is a foundational bodily right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Fundamental protection affecting all citizens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A permanent, structural protection in Athenian law.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes historical reforms of Solon.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the abolition of debt-slavery as a historical event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not conceptually exclude the possibility of slavery.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from magistracy/office is a loss of political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad range of administrative and executive roles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Usually lasts as long as one remains in the Thetic class.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Property class (Thetes) is durable but not immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to Solon's division of the population.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the exclusion from office as a matter of law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a Thete does not mean 'office-less' by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Holding the office of Treasurer involves significant power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to one highly important administrative office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Eligibility persists as long as one is in the top class.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The Pentacosiomedimnus class is a durable wealth bracket.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Aristotle explicitly notes the law is 'still in force'.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites an existing law as absolute proof of the practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealthy status is not defined by being a Treasurer.
